intro: Neocupressus goveniana, also known as Goven's cypress, is a plant species that belongs to the cypress family (Cupressaceae) and is native to California, USA. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Neocupressus goveniana include essential oils, terpenes, and phenolic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
